post.content: Dijkstra doesn\'t work with negative values but, Shouldn\'t it be bellman ford or something?

For the followup:\n1. If we\'re allowed to move in all the 4 directions but the matrix will be having only the `+ve weights` or at least `no -ve cycles` guarantee is there then we can leverage normal `Djikstra` algorithm to derive the result. But in the case of `-ve cycles` this won\'t work.\n2. Assuming that we should `only include a cell once` in the answer then we can leverage modified `Djikstra` where we keep a `visited` set per path and push it along with the currSum on the `heap`. This allows us to avoid `cycles` even in the presence of -ve weights and we can arrive at the solution with heavy `memory` usage though.
How soon after the phone screen did you get the callback?
Wasn\'t it true that **Dijkstra** doesn\'t work with negative values?
Thanks for giving the above solution. But can you provide credit that you copy pasted the solution from chatgpt
